title: Don Juan’s Reckless Daughter at 50
description: Much misunderstood at the time, the album received mixed reviews when it was released in 1977 at the height of punk. It lost Mitchell her commercial viability. “The Tenth World”, “Jericho” and “Off Night Backstreet” were all released as singles in the UK but did nothing. As the years have gone by, though, the album has grown in stature and reputation. What before seemed artistically muddled now appears as a clear act of supreme self expression. In its ‘carnivalesque’ aspect, Don Juan re
hook: Joni Mitchell's misunderstood masterpiece reconsidered: how a late-70s album lost commercial viability but gained eternal depth.

title: The Alysa Liu Effect
description: She proves that an Olympic gold-medalist figure skater can be strong, warm to her competitors, and salty all at the same time.
hook: Olympic figure skater Alysa Liu embodies a new archetype: strong, warm to rivals, and refreshingly salty—breaking the mold.

title: Forgotten Words: The Raincoats’ Three Lives
description:
Audrey Golden's book, Shouting Out Loud: Lives of The Raincoats, reframes punk history, making space for voices excluded from the usual male-dominated narratives of rock history

Raincoats, 1981, photo by Shirley O'Loughlin

Audrey Golden opens Shouting Out Loud by rejecting a premise most biographers accept without q
hook: The Raincoats' punk history reframed through excluded voices, centering women's contributions to a male-dominated narrative.

title: He Was a Legendary Newsroom Colleague. Turned Out He Had a Secret Past
description:
How an unexpected email led me to crack the mystery of Charles Saunders

The post He Was a
hook: A legendary newsroom colleague's secret past revealed—identity, reinvention, and the stories we construct about each other.

title: “I Really Wanted to Create a Chekhovian World”: Kornél Mundruczó on the Amy Adams-starring Berlinale Competition Film At the Sea
description: Drumroll: Amy Adams stares at you. It’s intense—not haunting, but certainly not inviting. The camera pulls away, and it’s her character Laura who’s playing the drums. It’s daytime, there’s
hook: Filmmaker Kornél Mundruczó on creating Chekhovian worlds: atmosphere as meaning, grace in intimate human moments.

title: In praise of crying at the cinema
description: The dark of the matinee provides a safe space for emotional outpouring.
hook: The cinema's dark space as emotional sanctuary—why crying at movies matters, aesthetically and psychologically.

title: Michael Pollan says AI may 'think' — but it will never be conscious
description: "Consciousness is under siege," says author Michael Pollan. His new book, A World Appears, explores consciousness on both a personal and technological level.
hook: Michael Pollan explores consciousness amid AI: a deeper meditation on what makes us human, beyond productivity rhetoric.
